Specific human voice vs committee 4/10 — Mostly sounds like content-mill output with occasional human flashes ('herding cats', 'hangry mutinies'), but the framing, TL;DR, and FAQ format scream template.

suggestedPatch.target: A real-world, day-by-day 7-day Oahu itinerary for friend groups: grounded in actual places, targeted for group organisers, and structured for minimal chaos.
suggestedPatch.before: A real-world, day-by-day 7-day Oahu itinerary for friend groups: grounded in actual places, targeted for group organisers, and structured for minimal chaos.
suggestedPatch.after: I've planned this Oahu week twice for friend groups—here's the version that survived contact with reality, hangovers, and one missed flight.

evidence: Genuine opinions vs safe claims 5/10 — Some real takes (rentals beat hotels at 4+, skip swimming in winter North Shore), but mostly hedged generalities.

suggestedPatch.target: Oahu is group- and family-friendly: beaches, parks, and safe neighborhoods.
suggestedPatch.before: Oahu is group- and family-friendly: beaches, parks, and safe neighborhoods.
suggestedPatch.after: Oahu handles both, but family-oriented beaches (Ko Olina) bore adult groups—stick to Kailua and North Shore if nobody's under 12.
suggestedPatch.rationale: Humanization axis "Genuine opinions vs safe claims" scored 5/10: Some real takes (rentals beat hotels at 4+, skip swimming in winter North Shore), but mostly hedged generalities.

suggestedPatch.before: Most groups fizzle at the logistics step, vague ideas, late polls, debates over car rentals, and last-second dinners.
suggestedPatch.after: Most groups die at the logistics step: vague ideas, a poll nobody answers, and one person quietly booking their own flight out of spite.
suggestedPatch.rationale: Humanization axis "Unexpected phrasings" scored 5/10: 'Herding cats,' 'hangry mutinies,' and 'Rainbow Drive-In plate lunch for the plane' land; most other phrasing is generic travel-blog.

evidence: Point of view 6/10 — Has a mild POV (rentals > hotels, book essentials ahead) but mostly surveys options evenly.

suggestedPatch.target: Waikiki: easy for first-timers, steps to food and main beaches. Ala Moana: better value, easier for relaxed group hangouts. Kailua: only practical if you get a rental; ideal for groups set on kayaking/beach mornings.
suggestedPatch.before: Waikiki: easy for first-timers, steps to food and main beaches. Ala Moana: better value, easier for relaxed group hangouts. Kailua: only practical if you get a rental; ideal for groups set on kayaking/beach mornings.
suggestedPatch.after: Skip Waikiki unless it's your first Hawaii trip—Ala Moana rentals win on every axis except being able to stumble to the beach drunk.
suggestedPatch.rationale: Humanization axis "Point of view" scored 6/10: Has a mild POV (rentals > hotels, book essentials ahead) but mostly surveys options evenly.

suggestedPatch.before: Oahu’s seasonal swings are real, pick wrong, and half the group’s whingeing.
